honestly the design of GDoll's brain is genius. Unironically.
not just the brain, the real doll features sell it

That it's been split into two hemispheres means the corpus callosum was severed. Anatomically that structure is responsible for cross-communication between the halves of the whole brain. Historically, in medical cases where its severance has been documented, the subjects therein were "prone to unusual behaviors," as though they were suddenly dealing with two separate patterns of thought at once. A good portion of them became erratic and unstable.
GDoll being the culmination of Doll's apathy towards humanity's fallacies and ineffectual efforts is displayed literally, prominently, dare I say even *focally* in her split brain.
All the genius, none of the connection.

Her body reminds me of Greek sculptures. It isn't exaggerated.
those sculptures are exaggerated though
*and* they were intentionally lurid
despite popular belief
It's body-as-figure in my perspective, less body-as-object.
there was a very strong sensuality to most of them
it's definitely like a love of the human form thing but that doesn't imply a separation from sexuality

The concept was not to become aroused at the sight of them, though. They were like icons. Ideals carved in stone as proof of passage and honor. Like the Herms of Greek origin. Erect phallic statuettes used to mark the borders of a person's property.
to a degree but while they were meant as icons i think they were also made to represent an ideal sexual form too
aside from the small dicks which despite popular belief weren't actually considered more attractive iirc

I'm not saying that people *shouldn't* be getting aroused at the sexual subcontext of art like that. There isn't really a should when it comes to this sort of thing. I personally just don't think they were designed with "this is sexually attractive and will arouse people" as the forefront consideration during their creation.
iirc it basically went along the lines of "it's more aesthetic and seemly in public" more than an actual sexual preference
i definitely think it was at least a part of it
We're agreeing with each other, we're just using different words~
especially since sexual undertones are often used to highlight and enhance other aspects
oh totally.
I don't think GDoll is sexless.
I think it's more of a commentary of how in her case, body is secondary to mind.

like while i'm not enough of an autist to lie to you and tell you Monogatari only uses sexuality for intellectual reasons it is in fact partially used as a weapon to make the series more tense and stressful

but the actual gameplay loop is fantastic
I started with world so to me Rise felt fine usually
i will once again repeat that i thin GU and Frontier are the best games in the franchise
and both games are incredibly easy to setup and will run on legit everything
see i think going from World to Rise is actually one of the most noticeable dropoffs
yeah I want to emulate the older ones eventually
sound design, hitstop, camera utilization
it's all pretty weak in Rise for the most part
but i notice this kind of thing more than most
i recommend 4U as your first oldgen game
it feels the most like World of any title and it's the most polished
and it has next to no pace breakers after the first couple gathering quests
once you fight your first large monster you're never really made to stop hunting monsters for anything
it respects your time

embeds.title: weekly video game news week 18 pt 3 of 52 2026 #gaming #news

content: I think them changing teostra’s particle effects I enjoyed shit in world used to hurt my eyes
Because if it's true then that's just unbelievably funny
Rise does really cool particle effects (usually, some are fucking awful)
the grind aspect of the older games for plants and stuff did sound like a huge turnoff. they did a great job with world QoL
but a lot of other things fall short
tbh World QoL is uhhhh way overblown
the grind in 4th gen is pretty similar
and 2nd gen the grind is less pace breaking because the games themselves are slower
oh okay, maybe big changes were just attributed to world
MonHun started out a lot less actiony
really the biggest change to grinding is that you can pick up items while moving
but the material drop rates are pretty similar between games
4U also actually has stronger passive income in the early game
the farm and its other facilities associated with it are stronger at a base level
late game it's weaker than in world but you really need it the most in the early game
my unironic take is that as the series goes on it kinda stops understanding why some things used to be okay
a lot of the QoL is in the wrong places
4U the variety in mons seemed crazy to me that was big reason I wanted to play
yeah 4U doesn't have the biggest roster in the series but the monster variety is pretty fucking tight
GU has more stuff as a whole but it's much less polished
infamously progression can be kinda lame at points
i like GU more because the areas that are unpolished don't bother me
like going back to world a lot of them felt samey, I’d get hate for saying that though lol. The variety in rise is noticeably better
nah you're right
World monster roster is like
genuinely one of the weakest in the whole series
Rise roster is infinitely better
the issue is vanilla Rise was *really* fucking easy
Sunbreak kicked it up to where it needed to be
but vanilla was like zero friction
fun fact outside of the TU postgame monsters Wilds is actually even easier
yeah I can see that. I only did the wilds demo I noticed the early mons don’t even knock you out of animations
9 YEARS AGO?
like you can just whale on them ignoring their moveset
yeah pretty much
I was 9 when this came out wtf
Rise is actually based because despite all the parries it's actually more like oldgen than world is in terms of actual gameplay loop
you've probably seen people describe MonHun gameplay as "turn-based" before
the parry hate felt so overblown to me lol
Rise did a really good job at emphasizing that
basically monsters get longer strings of attacks with defined recovery at the end
the primal malzeno or whatever I felt that for sure, I really enjoyed that actually
and the parries exist as a way to let you keep your turn even during monster attacks
i don't like Parries in MonHun outright
like he wasn’t that tanky you just had to live long enough to get your damage in when you could
but Rise utilized them really well
Wilds doesn't
Wilds suffers from parries being super low risk and low investment
and monsters generally not being capable of competing with the already even stronger than Rise hunters
Rise despite its many flaws is pretty smart in its design
it just stumbles in places
part of why you see me shit on World is it doesn't really do a good job at having that turn-based structure
~~and Clutch Claw is fucking atrocious lmao~~
yeah I didn’t get good enough in rise I was just parry spamming everyone to death
i love World btw
I’d take wirebug over clutch claw any day
and same
but i'm painfully aware of its failings
i'm very critical on things i love
if Wilds was just a random action game i played i wouldn't mind it
but as a MonHun title i really don't like it
and i don't believe it *should* be divorced from the previous games
that's an argument some people put up that i disagree with
yeah thats valid. I feel like average mh fan I talked with doesn’t criticize, they like legitimately hate the games atp lol
tbh i kinda get it
while i love World and Rise i think they were a step in the wrong direction
and they keep going further and further in that direction
they just happen to be games that have merit still
*technically* i started with Tri but realistically i started with World
yeah I think worlds success was moreso people being exposed to the series the first time, not them constantly making games easier or more accessible
and getting into the franchise as a whole kinda made me realize i don't actually like the new direction for MonHun that much
i happen to like most of the new games
but not the direction they're taking with them
like wilds I was pretty hyped for, but then all the performance problems and seeing base game didn’t have much and it pretty easy, I just felt like may aswell wait for the dlc
so i can understand why someone who's been a fan way longer would feel genuine resentment and hate for the series
yeah same
i was hyped as fuck and then i played the whole thing
and it sucks
it's so painfully mediocre and genuinely afraid of player friction

it went from being a game about being the small guy fighting an unreasonable enemy and the fantasy of overcoming great odds to being a power fantasy about being a badass guy with a giant sword who kills dragons
notice how the player always benefits from the terrain but the monsters never really get to benefit like you do
first fumble from capcom I’ve seen in awhile. World was like a global phenomenon, Wilds kinda just came and left
Lagiacrus is one of the best examples
in 3rd gen when he went in the water he was ***WAY*** scarier than on land
and you were actively at a disadvantage
in Wilds you basically enter a QTE that's incredibly simple to learn
it’s weird, its like they’re afraid of casuals giving up and dropping the game, but the hardware was so demanding the average casual can’t even run the game well
yeah
i made it into the beta for the mobile game, Outlanders
and completely unironically i like it more than Wilds
it has a lot of the same failings but it offers much more interesting builds and smaller but more focused playstyles
i've been very vocal about wanting smaller movesets for hunters but more moveset customization
and Outlanders basically does that
oh yeah I’ll try that out too when it launches. Another thing that’d be cool for them to work on again is the tracking
